item.title: [SAPD] Assault — SW LOOP 410 / CARMONA PASS (SOUTH)

item.title: [SAPD] Burglary Vehicle — 2000 HACKAMORE LN (WEST)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary Vehicle — Incident #SAPD-2026-0926741 | WEST Division | 7/8/2026 12:35:10 PM

item.title: Large language model guides discovery of catalysts for clean energy tech
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: large language model — Designing high-performance catalysts is essential for cleaner energy technologies, but the behavior of multi-element modern catalyst materials is difficult to predict. In a new study, researchers at Tohoku University with international collaborators developed a collaborative framework that combines large language models with lab experiments to accelerate the discovery of high-entropy alloy catalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction, a key process in fuel cells.

item.title: This rare British butterfly looks familiar, but its genome tells a very different story
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — The British swallowtail butterfly (Papilio machaon britannicus) is the U.K.'s only native swallowtail and its largest native butterfly. It's instantly recognizable by its striking light yellow-and-black wings, with twin tail-like markings splashed in bold blue and red. Habitat for this endangered species has largely been limited to the damp bogs and marshes of eastern England, especially the iconic Norfolk Broads, where its life cycle is closely tied to a single host plant, milk-parsley.

item.title: Social norms can accelerate or undermine climate action, new model finds
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — A new mathematical model suggests that social norms may be just as important as economics in determining how the world responds to climate change. The research shows that efforts to reduce emissions in one region can unintentionally influence climate action elsewhere, with consequences that could either strengthen or weaken global progress.

item.title: Oil prices rise sharply after Iran launches attacks on tankers near strait of Hormuz
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Brent crude benchmark rose to more than $78 a barrel, its steepest increase since ceasefire beganOil markets have recorded the sharpest price rise in nearly two months after a series of attacks on fossil fuel tankers near the strait of Hormuz led Donald Trump to declare that the ceasefire deal with Iran was “over”.Brent, the global crude benchmark rose by 5% on Wednesday to more than $78 (£58) a barrel, the highest price since the US and Iran agreed the ceasefire while negotiating an end to the

item.title: [SAPD] Burglary — 1100 NW 23RD ST (CENTRAL)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary — Incident #SAPD-2026-0925681 | CENTRAL Division | 7/8/2026 8:28:27 AM

item.title: [SAPD] Burglary Vehicle — 6400 S NEW BRAUNFELS AVE (SOUTH)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary Vehicle — Incident #SAPD-2026-0925721 | SOUTH Division | 7/8/2026 8:40:42 AM

item.title: Trump plan to appease MAHA’s fury backfired big time
item.description: [Small Farm & Urban Agriculture] Keyword: regenerative agriculture — On a 200-acre farm and cattle ranch in Bandera, Texas, Mollie Engelhart grows organic produce, sells raw milk, and writes a daily column about the power of regenerative agriculture. She’s a farmer and a Make America Healthy Again mom who doesn’t like being called a MAHA mom. She prefers to think of herself as “MAHA-aligned.” In May, Engelhart opened her ranch to a couple hundred pro-MAHA politicians, activists, and leaders for a two-day MAHA farming retreat. Department of Health and Human Servi

item.title: Tiny Jurassic bird reveals a key step in bird evolution
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — The transition from a lumbering, heavy dinosaur body to the flight-adapted bird body plan is one of many fascinating episodes in evolutionary history. Working out how this massive transformation took place relies heavily on fossil records, especially of transitional species. A study of a newly identified Jurassic bird published in the journal Science Advances is providing fresh insights into a tail adaptation that helps birds fly.

item.title: A spirit of resilience helps Venezuelans face healthcare challenges after the quakes
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — The economic collapse of Venezuela has pushed its healthcare system to the brink. The disaster is now adding to the stress.

item.title: [SAPD] Missing Person/Runaway — 200 NOLAN ST (DOWNTOWN)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Missing Person/Runaway — Incident #SAPD-2026-0925251 | DOWNTOWN Division | 7/8/2026 4:36:43 AM

item.title: Hotter, drier weather could double water bills in some US cities, study finds
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Hotter, drier weather threatens to double water bills by midcentury in some cities, according to a Stanford-led study. The research, published in Nature Sustainability, is the first to comprehensively model how climate change, infrastructure investment and household water demand can combine to compound an already growing affordability crisis.

item.title: Climate Change Is Helping an Invasive Predator Wreak Havoc on Iconic Alaskan Fish
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — WILLOW, Alaska—Corey Ercolani pulled a northern pike from a gillnet and slit its belly with a knife. Inside its guts lay fresh evidence of a growing biological crime: a dead juvenile salmon. A coho, or silver salmon, to be exact. A technician with the Alaska Department of Fish and Game, Ercolani had set the net […]

item.title: The oldest deliberately collected fossil ichthyosaur was discovered in Roman Britain around 1,800 years ago
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Around 1,800 years ago, a fossilized spinal bone lay on the windswept beaches of Roman Britain until a curious passerby picked it up and carried it far away, only to drop it in a pit.

item.title: The great carbon capture con: behold the wasted billions Burnham could claw back | George Monbiot
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — There are far better ways to tackle climate breakdown, but successive governments have chosen to listen to the fossil fuel companies insteadThe new prime minister will be looking for money? Well, here’s £21.7bn lying on the ground. The government could cancel its deranged, disastrous carbon capture and storage (CCS) programme at no cost to public welfare: in fact, it would greatly reduce the harm we will suffer.Sorry, did I say £21.7bn? That’s the figure the government has been putting in its pr

item.title: New weight loss pill beats oral Ozempic in major trial
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: blood sugar — A new once-daily weight-loss pill called orforglipron delivered better weight loss and blood sugar improvements than the leading oral semaglutide in a major clinical trial. The tablet could offer a more convenient alternative to injectable drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy because it doesn't require refrigeration or special timing with meals. It's also cheaper to manufacture, which could expand access globally.

item.title: Women and university graduates in Australia most at risk of losing jobs to AI, report finds
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — Those with high levels of vocational training, including tradespeople, are least exposed to AI displacement, according to government reviewGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastArtificial intelligence has yet to cause widespread job losses but the federal government has warned that telemarketers, advertising staff and accountants are among the occupations “most exposed” to being replaced by the technology.According to a first-of-its-kind national report, people in the more e

item.title: Warehouse store plants corridor’s first retail flag as TxDOT works to keep up with growth
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Construction on the busy interchange continues with a flyover underway amid the ensuing commercial and residential growth.Warehouse store plants corridor’s first retail flag as TxDOT works to keep up with growth was first posted on July 8, 2026 at 5:00 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: How climate change affects interactions between owls and their prey
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — A study published in Ecography has assessed how climate change may be destabilizing interactions between predators and prey in the wild—specifically, how owl–prey interactions have responded to environmental variability and resource availability over 24 years in the semi-arid ecosystem of Bosque Fray Jorge National Park in Chile.

item.title: UK museums at 'sharp end' of climate change challenge
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — As visitors peered at skeletons and preserved animals in a London zoological museum during a UK heat wave, staff focused on a different attraction: a computer screen glowing with red and green temperature indicators.

item.title: Health risk fears for storks in Europe over ‘junk food’ from landfill
item.description: [Foraging & Wild Plants] Keyword: foraging — Rubbish dumps can expose birds to contaminants, raising questions over whether landfill foraging helps or harmsStorks are gaining weight from a diet of literal junk, according to research that suggests the previously disappearing birds face potential health risks as a result of increasingly eating from rubbish dumps.Landfill offers what appear to be quick and convenient meals for white stork populations in Europe. But new research suggests they may be gaining a short-term energy boost at the cos

item.title: [SAPD] Assault — 2100 CUPPLES RD (SOUTH)

item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary in Progress — Incident #SAPD-2026-0924817 | CENTRAL Division | 7/8/2026 12:35:02 AM

item.title: How tall and short trees can coexist in old growth forests
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: old growth forest — Forests are shaped by light competition. The trees that grow the tallest have access to the most sunlight, blocking the rays and rendering the shaded space around them inhospitable to shorter trees below. In this stem exclusion phase of forest succession, the shorter trees often die. Yet scientists have observed that in old-growth forests, trees of vastly different sizes successfully coexist, proving that reaching the top of the canopy is not the sole winning strategy for survival in a forest en

item.title: Global warming, increasing wildfire risk threaten viability of some California winery regions
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: global warming — The U.S. is the fourth-largest wine-producing country by output volume, and approximately 80% of its production occurs in California. Ever since the 19th century, California's premier wine-growing regions have been the Napa Valley and Sonoma County, thanks to their favorable microclimate. But grape yield and quality are very sensitive to the local environment, which means the climate crisis could shake up California's wine industry.

item.title: [SAPD] Assault In Progress — 1200 ARIZONA ASH DR (PRUE)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Assault In Progress — Incident #SAPD-2026-0924272 | PRUE Division | 7/7/2026 9:17:34 PM

item.title: Where rivers face collapse: New tool shows where conservation dollars can do most good
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: biodiversity loss — Freshwater ecosystems are under growing pressure worldwide, but conservation resources are limited. A framework developed by IIASA researchers and partners can help identify where conservation could prevent biodiversity loss and where restoration efforts are likely to have the greatest ecological impact across the United States and Europe.

item.title: Bexar County moves to spend $10M on South Beach Pavilion project for Defense Health Agency

item.title: Satellite record reveals US tidal wetland productivity rose 6% in 20 years
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Carbon sequestration, climate regulation, biodiversity support and shoreline protection: These are all benefits provided by tidal wetlands. As the climate changes, the amount of carbon captured by these vital ecosystems may be changing as well.

item.title: Unique fossil record of marine mollusks helps scientists predict extinction risk—before it's too late
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Scientists are increasingly worried we may be witnessing the start of the "sixth mass extinction"—the first to be caused by human activities.

item.title: Spurs get permission for more international games, plus up to 2 Austin ‘home’ games per season
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Bexar County leaders granted the San Antonio Spurs permission to play four regular-season home games outside of the Frost Bank Center during each of the 2026-2027 and 2027-2028 NBA seasons.Spurs get permission for more international games, plus up to 2 Austin ‘home’ games per season was first posted on July 7, 2026 at 4:29 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, th

item.title: Record Heat Drives Wildfires in Europe, Forces Thousands to Evacuate
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Wildfires are tearing through southern France and parts of Spain, Portugal and Greece, forcing thousands of people to evacuate from their homes. The wildfires follow a record-breaking June heat wave across the continent that caused thousands of deaths, strained healthcare facilities, dried out acres of land and is slated to continue with triple-digit temperatures in […]

item.title: Trump Tells Big Tech He’ll Fast-Track Fossil Fuel Plants to Power AI
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — The AI development race has resulted in plans to build or expand 74 methane gas plants across the country.

item.title: Justice Department scrambles to save Todd Blanche from himself
item.description: [Government Accountability] Keyword: FOIA — The Justice Department is currently trying to save acting Attorney General Todd Blanche from a lawsuit filed by the government watchdog group American Oversight over the release of files related to Jeffrey Epstein.In June, the group filed suit with a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request involving “all email communications sent or received by Todd Blanche and containing both a key term from Column A and a key term from Column B,” and “all text messages and messages on messaging platforms ..

item.title: CineFestival returns with its largest showcase yet of San Antonio filmmakers

item.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.

item.title: Ancient jaw wound reveals possible violence in Homo sapiens 90,000 years ago
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Violence, the care of injured or ill individuals, and funerary behavior are among the most challenging aspects of the human past to reconstruct. A study published in Scientific Reports and led by researchers from the Centro Nacional de Investigación sobre la Evolución Humana (CENIEH), in collaboration with colleagues from Tel Aviv University, provides new insights into these questions through the analysis of Qafzeh 25, a fossil human from Qafzeh Cave (Israel), dated to between 92,000 and 145,000

item.title: Obamacare Enrollment Declines Driven by Subsidy Cuts, Not Removing Fraudulent Enrollees
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — Millions of low- and middle-income Americans are losing health coverage because the Trump administration allowed the subsidies that made it affordable to lapse, according to a new Public Citizen analysis of government data. The analysis contradicts claims by Trump administration health officials that removal of fraudulent enrollees is responsible for declining Obamacare enrollment.Enrollment in the Affordable Care Act marketplace decreased from 22.3 million people in 2025 to an estimated 17.5 mi

item.title: [SAPD] Burglary Vehicle — 70 NE LOOP 410 (NORTH)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary Vehicle — Incident #SAPD-2026-0920979 | NORTH Division | 7/7/2026 9:09:35 AM

item.title: Nearly 20% of new moms have anxiety or depression, but a promising psychedelic treatment is on the horizon
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: clinical trial — The psychedelic treatment is moving through the FDA clinical trial process in Colorado and elsewhere.

item.title: There's no treatment designed for the Ebola strain ravaging DRC. But now there's hope
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: clinical trial — The strain of the virus that's responsible for the current outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures. Three new clinical trials could provide possible breakthroughs.

item.title: Cheetah chases inspire researchers to make a biologically accurate video game
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: citizen science — Movement data from wild predator–prey encounters and controlled human catch-tag games have been combined to create realistic simulations of high-intensity movement dynamics and energetics—before being transformed into a publicly accessible video game. This game uses a citizen science approach to data collection and is helping to further our understanding of the role of movement decision-making and fatigue in life-or-death encounters.

item.title: Hawaiʻi island spinner dolphins are producing fewer calves
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — Unexpectedly low calf numbers within the spinner dolphin population off Hawaiʻi Island were revealed in a study led by scientists with the Marine Mammal Research Program (MMRP) at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, along with national and international collaborators. Their research, published in Endangered Species Research, determined that calves make up only about 9% of the population, a stark contrast to the 17% expected in a healthy, stable population, raising urgent concerns about the long-

item.title: Citizen science could grow beyond data collection under 10 proposed recommendations
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Citizen science is booming and could play a much greater role in tackling climate change, reef health, biodiversity and public health challenges by involving members of the public throughout the research process, according to a new study.

item.title: Inside GEF’s Blended Finance Push: Turning Public Money Into Private Capital Leverage
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: ecosystem restoration — For most of the Eighth Global Environmental Facility (GEF) Assembly last month, the atmosphere inside Samarkand’s sprawling Congress Centre echoed a growing confidence of global environmental policymakers. Delegates darted between plenary halls and side events discussing biodiversity targets, climate adaptation and ecosystem restoration. PowerPoint charts displayed shrinking forests. Investment bankers touted new financing tools. Smartly […]

item.title: As climate change damages streets and highways, the road ahead may be expensive
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Heat waves are becoming more common and intense as a result of climate change — and roads are suffering as a result. Are the nation's roads up to meeting the challenge of a warmer, wetter future?

item.title: ‘You never truly quit’: how RuneScape survived to 25 – and beyond
item.description: [Tabletop RPG & D&D] Keyword: role-playing game — The massively multiplayer online role-playing game has grown into a virtual social space and part of daily life for thousands of playersIn a small stone chapel, on the edgelands of a medieval wilderness, two women are getting married. The attenders are draped in rainbow capes, glowing armour and top hats. A scantily clad, muscular man with angel wings officiates the ceremony. Over the heads of the two brides hover the words “I do” in bright yellow text. This is RuneScape, a massively multiplayer

item.title: Bumblebees exposed to up to 7 times as much toxic metal as honeybees
item.description: [Foraging & Wild Plants] Keyword: foraging — Bumblebees collect up to seven times the amount of toxic heavy metals as honeybees even when foraging in the same environment, new research from the University of Cambridge has found. Exposure to these metals can affect everything from their ability to forage for food to their ability to reproduce.

item.title: Alamo Colleges District launches one-stop hub for workforce training programs
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — The Alamo Technical Institute is an effort to streamline access to advising, career coaching and enrollment into short-term credential programs.Alamo Colleges District launches one-stop hub for workforce training programs was first posted on July 7, 2026 at 5:00 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Please contact me at hell

item.title: Deep Dive Into Local Data Shows Diversity of Rural Health Issues in Georgia
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Digging into data can help policymakers and healthcare providers understand the challenges facing rural residents in Georgia, according to researchers at a nonprofit healthcare research group. The new study from the Parkland Center for Clinical Innovation digs down to the micro level to look at the health vulnerabilities faced by Georgia’s 663,000 rural residents. The […] The post Deep Dive Into Local Data Shows Diversity of Rural Health Issues in Georgia appeared first on The Daily Yonde

item.title: Climate change means an earlier spring, which can be disorienting and threatening for migrating birds
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Spring migration has taken flight, but with rising temperatures and shifting seasons, birds are adjusting when and how they migrate to keep up with a rapidly warming climate. Morgan Tingley, a professor of ecology and evolutionary biology at the University of California, Los Angeles, studies the effects of climate change on birds.

item.title: City of San Antonio is the new owner of Hemisfair property slated for Project Marvel development
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The city bought the building with funds from the Spurs which plans to redevelop the property for an arena district in downtown San Antonio.City of San Antonio is the new owner of Hemisfair property slated for Project Marvel development was first posted on July 6, 2026 at 6:05 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: Modeling reveals Sydney's 1789 smallpox outbreak killed as many as 220,000 indigenous Australians
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Groundbreaking modeling has revealed that as many as 220,000 Indigenous Australians died in a devastating 1789 smallpox outbreak that originated in Sydney, tracing its origins to the First Fleet. Soon after British ships arrived, smallpox swept through First Nations communities in the Sydney area, and many died as a result. The study found that the epidemic had a devastating demographic impact and ramifications for how First Nations people resisted colonization and their capacity to manage the c

item.title: [SAPD] Burglary in Progress — 100 BRANIFF (NORTH)

item.title: Here’s the full schedule for the Spurs public listening tour on the future arena and downtown sports district
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — A series of meeetings to gather input on Project Marvel starts July 13 and continues through July 23 at locations across San Antonio.Here’s the full schedule for the Spurs public listening tour on the future arena and downtown sports district was first posted on July 6, 2026 at 2:32 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement

item.title: Four new groups of indigenous cacao varieties discovered in Peru
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — A new genetic analysis of hundreds of cacao trees representing traditional Amazonian varieties grown on farms across Peru has revealed four previously unidentified, genetically distinct groups. Lambert Motilal, with colleagues from the Cocoa Research Center, The University of the West Indies, and Martha S. Calderon and Danilo E. Bustamante, with their colleagues from the Universidad Nacional Toribio Rodríguez de Mendoza, partnered to explore Peru's untapped cacao diversity.

item.title: Alpine butterflies track warming uphill, but habitat loss may pose bigger risk
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: habitat loss — A new study published in the journal Alpine Entomology has found that alpine butterflies in the Swiss National Park are closely matching the pace of local warming in their range shift to higher elevations.

item.title: Paleontologists make 'one in a million' discovery of soft tissue preserved in 450-million-year-old fossil
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Before the oldest dinosaur, before animals or even plants had expanded onto dry land, ancient relatives of starfish called crinoids, resembling stalked sea flowers, were among the first creatures to flourish in Earth's earliest coral reefs more than 450 million years ago. The study of fossilized crinoids helps scientists understand how these animals evolved and offers rare insight into the origins of complex life.

item.title: Big Banks Are Accelerating Their Investments in the Fossil Fuel Industry
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — International banks are financing the industry’s pivot to petrochemical products like plastics and fertilizers.

item.title: From north to south, Africa braces for volatile El Nino year
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — The World Meteorological Association warns that the El Nino climate phenomenon could bring drought, floods and displacement to parts of Africa later this year. Are governments prepared?

item.title: Judge rules against CPS Energy in $400M 2021 winter storm gas contract dispute
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — State District Judge Laura Salinas has ordered the municipally-owned utility CPS Energy to pay nearly $400 million to two Energy Transfer subsidiaries after ruling it breached contracts for natural gas purchased during the February 2021 winter storm.Judge rules against CPS Energy in $400M 2021 winter storm gas contract dispute was first posted on July 6, 2026 at 1:02 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading thi

item.title: [SAPD] Burglary — 21300 ENCINO COMMONS (NORTH)
item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Burglary — Incident #SAPD-2026-0916452 | NORTH Division | 7/6/2026 10:39:49 AM

item.title: From birdsong to galaxies: Unleashing the potential of citizen science
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: citizen science — Everyone could contribute dramatically more to scientific knowledge if given better recognition, administrative support and funding stability, new research has found. An international collaboration of 16 organizations used surveys and group discussions to identify key challenges, opportunities and best practices in citizen science projects.

item.title: Climate change is silencing the Pilbara barking gecko
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — New research from Monash University has provided the first comprehensive assessment of the Pilbara barking gecko (Underwoodisaurus seorsus) and revealed the species is facing a heightened risk of extinction under a rapidly warming climate.

item.title: Moving forests to save them: Here are the risks and rewards of assisted tree migration
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Climate change is shifting forest conditions faster than local tree species can adapt. Higher temperatures, more frequent droughts and less predictable winters can weaken species that have been established in a region for centuries. In light of this, one idea is gaining ground: assisted migration.

item.title: Climate change means an earlier spring, which can be disorienting and threatening for migrating birds
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Birds that migrate longer distances are the ones having the most trouble keeping up with climate change.

item.title: GB News co-owner ‘cashing in on climate chaos’ after leap in fossil fuel investments, critics say
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Exclusive: Campaigners argue news channel’s attacks on climate action ‘work in financial interests’ of Sir Paul MarshallThe hedge fund run by the co-owner of GB News almost tripled its investments in fossil fuel companies in the first quarter of 2026 to $2.8bn (£2.1bn), the Guardian can reveal.Critics have accused Sir Paul Marshall of “cashing in on climate chaos” and have claimed the news channel, which frequently attacks climate science and action, was “working in its owner’s financial interes

item.title: Wegovy weight-loss pill goes on sale on UK high street and online pharmacies
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: GLP-1 — Thousands of people begin receiving their first deliveries of once-a-day medication made by Novo NordiskBusiness live – latest updatesA once-a-day Wegovy weight-loss pill has gone on sale at high street and online pharmacies in the UK, but is not yet available on the NHS.Thousands of people began receiving their first deliveries of the pill, made by the Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk, on Monday. It contains the same active GLP-1 ingredient as the Wegovy jab, semaglutide, and is similarly effectiv

item.title: Scorching heat worsens suffering in Gaza’s displacement camps
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — Scorching heat worsens suffering in Gaza's displacement camps
